titleStmt.title: Telegram from the War Office to Samuel Cleland Davidson, 7 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Reginald H. Brade

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Telegram from the War Office in London to Samuel Cleland Davidson (1846-1921) reporting the death of his son, Captain James Davidson (1877-1916). Captain Davidson was serving with the Machine Gun Corps when he was killed in action in France on 1 July 1916. The War Office express their sympathy.Sir Samuel Cleland Davidson was an Irish inventor and founder of the Sirocco Engineering Works in Belfast. During the First World War the Sirocco Engineering Works manufactured and exported equipment for cooling, drying, dust collecting, heating, ventilating, pneumatic conveyance for the Royal and Merchant Navy. Sir Reginald H Brade (1864-1933) was the secretary of the War Office throughout the First World War.

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
OHMS war office London S.C. Davidson Seacount Bangor Co Down Deeply regret to inform you
that Capt J. S. Davidson
machine gun corps was killed
in action on 1st July
the army council expresses
their sympathy secy war office
